title: thalictrum foetidum subsp. foetidum
intro: Thalictrum foetidum subsp. foetidum, also known as the stinking meadow rue, is a plant species in the family Ranunculaceae characterized by its unpleasant odor. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Thalictrum foetidum subsp. foetidum include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use as an antiseptic to prevent wound infections. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made into teas, tinctures, or infusions to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is necessary to confirm its efficacy and safety.
